Frohnau, Berlin, north-eastern part
recordings.rmk: recording made immediately after XC1074971, featuring the neighbour male singing only a few dozen meters away; this bird singing a typical combination of pre-song and loud advertising song in about equal parts - very much different than its neighbour at the same time that was singing only the advertising part

recordings.loc: Frohnau, Berlin, north-eastern part
recordings.rmk: dawn song of a male, practically without pre-song, featuring only the most characteristic loud advertising part of the Blackcap song

recordings.loc: Waldgelände Frohnau, Berlin
recordings.rmk: song of a male recorded from ca. 3 m distance, first strophe with extremely long pre-song (1 min of it recorded, not even capturing the beginning of it), all following strophes with typical length of pre-song and final part

recordings.loc: Sármellék, Hévízi, Zala County
recordings.rmk: Not modified. In the Kis-Balaton marshland.

recordings.loc: Sisalde, Ames, A Coruña, Galicia
recordings.rmk: SNR 51 dB (ISO/ITU) Phrases with consistent gaps, some ending in incipient whistlings notes. Probably a transition between subsong to plastic song.

recordings.loc: Trail above Saint-Ursanne
recordings.rmk:
This bird was singing nonstop just a few feet away from me. This is the longest of several recordings I made of it in a period of about 15 minutes. Full eBird list can be found here:
https://ebird.org/checklist/S254338965

recordings.loc: Great Britain (near  Newport), Newport, Wales
recordings.rmk: Presumed male Blackcap singing it's beautiful song, even imitating a Song Thrush. Listen to the end of each verse for the distinct dialect found in the RSPB Newport Wetlands area.
